seoTitle: How to Add Outlook Calendar to Notion (Sync Guide)
metaDescription: Embed your Outlook calendar in Notion using the ICS sharing link, resize it to fit your page, and verify it updates in near real time as you add events.
intro: Switching between separate apps to check your schedule creates room for double-booking. This guide covers generating a shareable link from Outlook Web, embedding it as a live calendar view inside Notion, and verifying that new events sync correctly.

steps.heading: Open your Outlook sharing settings
steps.body: Log into Outlook Web, click the Settings gear icon, and navigate to the Calendar tab to find Shared calendars.

steps.heading: Publish your calendar
steps.body: Go to the Publish a Calendar section, select your schedule, set permissions to view all details, and click Publish.

steps.heading: Copy your link
steps.body: Copy the generated HTML link for basic viewing, or the ICS link for live syncing.

steps.heading: Embed it in Notion
steps.body: In your Notion workspace, go to the page where you want the calendar and type /embed.

steps.heading: Paste your link
steps.body: Paste the Outlook link into the field and click Embed link to add the calendar block.

steps.heading: Resize and verify
steps.body: Drag the embed's corners to fit your page layout, then add a test event in Outlook and refresh Notion to confirm it syncs.

faq.question: Does the embedded calendar update automatically?
faq.answer: The ICS link is designed for ongoing syncing, though the update frequency can lag slightly behind real time depending on caching.

faq.question: Can I embed more than one Outlook calendar in Notion?
faq.answer: Yes, you can repeat the embed process for each calendar you want to add as a separate block on your page.

faq.question: Is there an alternative to the embed block for syncing?
faq.answer: Yes, Notion's built-in connections center lets you link your Microsoft account directly for a more automated update pathway.

faq.question: Why doesn't my embedded calendar show private event details?
faq.answer: The visibility depends on the sharing permission you chose when publishing, so set it to view all details if you need full information visible.
